---
title: "1.6 Compound Assignment Operators"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Compound Assignment

### Update and assign in one step

Compound assignment operators are shortcuts such as:

::: {.tight-list}
- `+=`
- `-=`
- `*=`
- `/=`
- `%=`
:::

`x += 1` means the same as `x = x + 1`.

## Operator Equivalence

### Read the shortcut as expanded assignment

| Written out | Compound |
|---|---|
| `x = x + 1` | `x += 1` |
| `x = x - 1` | `x -= 1` |
| `x = x * 2` | `x *= 2` |
| `x = x / 2` | `x /= 2` |
| `x = x % 2` | `x %= 2` |

The operation happens first, then the new value is assigned back into the same variable.

## Increment and Decrement

### The most common short forms

Because `+1` and `-1` are so common, Java also provides:

::: {.tight-list}
- `x++` -> increment by 1
- `y--` -> decrement by 1
:::

For AP CSA, the most important reading is still the simple update idea: <span class="mark">current value -> change -> store again</span>.

## Prefix and Postfix

### Know the note, keep the AP usage simple

Real Java code may use `++x` or `x++`.

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">postfix</span>: use the current value first, then update
- <span class="term">prefix</span>: update first, then use the value
:::

The textbook explicitly notes that AP-style work stays with <span class="mark">clear, readable uses</span>, not tricky expression puzzles.

## Code Tracing

### Operators are easiest to learn by tracing

![Trace table example](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-6-compound-assignment-operators/traceTable.png){fig-align="center" width="26%"}

<span class="term">Code tracing</span> (代码跟踪) means stepping through the program line by line and recording how variables change.

This is the bridge from operator meaning to exam-style reasoning.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- shortcut-operator active code
- tracing multiple-choice checks
- <span class="term">1.6.1 Code Tracing Challenge and Operators Maze</span>
:::

The instructional emphasis is <span class="mark">not memorizing symbols in isolation</span>; it is predicting variable state accurately.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- expand `+=`, `-=`, `*=`, `/=`, and `%=` into full assignment form
- explain `++` and `--` as one-step updates
- trace variable values after multiple updates
- recognize that AP work avoids tricky prefix/postfix edge cases
- use a trace table when the code becomes hard to hold mentally
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
